title: Engineering technicians
description: Design, develop, test, and evaluate integrated systems for managing industrial production processes, including human work factors, quality control, inventory control, logistics and material flow, cost analysis, and production coordination. Design objects, facilities, and environments to optimize human well-being and overall system performance, applying theory, principles, and data regarding the relationship between humans and respective technology. Investigate and analyze characteristics of human behavior and performance as it relates to the use of technology. Design or plan protocols for equipment or processes to produce products meeting internal and external purity, safety, and quality requirements. (Based on 28 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients, vendors, staff, and management personnel regarding purchases, product and production specifications, manufacturing capabilities, or project status.
Recommend resolutions for identified deviations from established product or process standards.

Assess the usability characteristics of products and design or evaluate human work systems using human factors engineering and ergonomic principles to optimize usability, cost, quality, safety, or performance.

Collect data through direct observation of work activities or conduct tests, and analyze complex systems for further development, production, interoperability, or compatibility in various applications.
Apply modeling or quantitative analysis to forecast human decisions or behaviors, and analyze financial impacts of sustainable manufacturing processes.
Advocate for end users in collaboration with other professionals to integrate human factors requirements into operational hardware and establish optimized human-machine interfaces.
Investigate theoretical or conceptual issues related to human design considerations, and conduct research to evaluate solutions related to equipment design and procedures.
Design tests of finished products or processes to validate requirements and determine root causes of failures, recommending changes as necessary using statistical procedures.
Incorporate new manufacturing methods to improve operations and analyze opportunities or implement changes to enhance manufacturing processes or reduce costs.
Evaluate manufacturing processes for environmental sustainability, considering factors like emissions and pollution.
Investigate user needs through interviews or surveys to gather information on ergonomic factors and provide technical support to clients on improving workplace conditions.
Conduct functional, task, or anthropometric analyses utilizing checklists, surveys or measuring tools, and apply statistical methods for production standards.
Prepare reports summarizing human factors engineering activities, evaluations, or quality control objectives to communicate results or conclusions.
Conduct audits and analyze validation test data to ensure compliance with standards, preparing the necessary documentation for new manufacturing processes or systems.

Participate in the development, testing, or supervision of electronic or mechanical systems including modifications and operations of components, while providing user support and ensuring quality control.
Design layouts of equipment and workspaces or machines and components to achieve maximum operational efficiency while estimating production costs and resource requirements for management.
Provide training on new methods or ergonomic principles to production personnel and maintain knowledge on engineering advances through continuing education activities.
Receive and procure parts or materials, maintain inventory documentation, and prepare detailed reports on manufacturing performance or production schedules.
Assist engineers in designing, developing, testing, or manufacturing products, while coordinating project instructions and blueprints for technical accuracy.
Train users in task techniques, analyze and improve utility of personnel, materials, and other resources for operational efficiency.
Set up and maintain mechanical, hydraulic, or electric test equipment in accordance with engineering specifications and standards, and document test procedures and results using various tools.

Recommend and conduct tests or testing conditions based on designs, customer requirements, or industry standards to ensure test validity, including modifying and planning fabrication, assembly, and testing requirements.
Read and interpret blueprints, schematics, work specifications, drawings, or charts to draft detailed dimensional drawings and design layouts to ensure conformance to specifications.
Inspect or test parts, assemblies, or systems for defects or malfunctions, and analyze test data for automotive or electromechanical systems to ensure compliance with specifications.
Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or systems, and ensure proper installation and configuration of equipment such as instrumentation or automation systems.
Confer with engineering personnel regarding test procedures, equipment modifications, and results, and adjust or replace faulty components as needed.
Operate, calibrate, and troubleshoot test setups and equipment, including testing performance under operational conditions and analyzing results to meet specifications.
Establish and maintain inventory, documentation, and service records related to testing, equipment maintenance, and overall project activities.
Train and direct personnel on the installation, use, and maintenance of robotic systems or other electromechanical equipment to enhance operational effectiveness.
Inspect and evaluate installation sites and systems to ensure compliance with safety standards, specifications, and to identify potential energy-saving measures.
Analyze energy consumption patterns and usage data to recommend strategies for improving energy efficiency within building or process systems.
Identify health or safety issues related to planned projects, and educate customers on energy efficiency and related topics based on field data analysis.
Design and develop information databases that include geographic or topographic data to assist with engineering and project planning tasks.
Monitor and compare ongoing project activities with applicable standards to ensure accuracy and compliance with requirements throughout construction and installation processes.
Conduct additional analyses, including statistical studies or cost estimations for sustainability initiatives, while applying engineering principles and techniques.
Evaluate design improvements or required modifications based on test data or observations, ensuring overall system performance meets designated objectives.
Specify, coordinate, or conduct quality-assurance programs and engage with contractors to ensure compliance with contracts and improve construction outcomes.
Document and analyze site factors along with scales and technical dimensions, as well as assisting with project stakings to guide construction processes appropriately.
Research, combine existing information, and evaluate test results to ensure appropriate measures are taken at project sites to meet structural and safety regulations.
Develop plans for installation or construction reflecting energy efficiency technologies and methods in accordance with building regulations or project specifications.
Utilize computer systems and software for simulations or design drawings, and maintain accurate electronic records of engineering projects or activities.
Collect field data related to energy, installation, or testing and recommend changes or improvements based on practical observations.

Prepare audit reports containing energy analysis results or recommendations for energy cost savings, and inspect newly installed energy-efficient equipment to ensure proper installation and performance according to specifications. Conduct inspections using survey instruments, metering devices, tape measures, or test equipment; and monitor construction activities to ensure compliance with environmental regulations. Inspect facilities or installations to evaluate their environmental impact, and conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify problems such as asbestos or poor air quality. Assess nature and extent of fire conditions and inspect buildings to locate hazardous conditions and fire code violations. Examine lifting or conveying devices to ensure safety and proper functioning. Use various methods to analyze evidence, prepare reports of investigation results, and review building plans or blueprints for safety compliance.

emerging_tasks.task: Assess systems to identify and quantify risk factors.
Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Evaluate the energy performance of buildings using modeling software.
Inspect structures to determine cause and origin of damage.
Operate drones for site surveying and inspection, providing detailed aerial views of project sites.
Use drone technology to capture aerial images or videos for creating detailed and accurate maps.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
